requirements.summary: Update TypeScript types to include Shopify integration fields in SiteSettings and mutation payloads
acceptanceCriteria: SiteSettings TypeScript type includes shopifyStoreDomain, shopifyStorefrontAccessToken, and shopifyEnabled
updateSiteSettings mutation payload includes these new fields
No TypeScript type errors related to the new fields

file_operations.description: Add shopifyStoreDomain (string), shopifyStorefrontAccessToken (string), and shopifyEnabled (boolean) fields to the SiteSettings interface to match the backend interface definition

Update the updateSiteSettings mutation payload type to include the new Shopify fields (shopifyStoreDomain, shopifyStorefrontAccessToken, shopifyEnabled) ensuring they are passed to the backend updateSiteSettings method

requirements.summary: Add Shopify Integration subsection to SiteSettingsSection component for configuring Shopify credentials
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Shopify Integration' subsection appears in the Site Settings tab of the Admin page
An enable/disable toggle for the Shopify integration is rendered
Input fields for Shopify Store Domain and Storefront Access Token are rendered
Fields are pre-populated with current values loaded from getSiteSettings on mount
Clicking Save persists values via updateSiteSettings and shows a success toast
If save fails, an error toast is displayed

file_operations.description: Add a 'Shopify Integration' subsection below the hero banner configuration with: a checkbox/switch for shopifyEnabled, text input for shopifyStoreDomain (placeholder: 'mystore.myshopify.com'), and text input for shopifyStorefrontAccessToken (type password for security). Bind all fields to the siteSettings state loaded from getSiteSettings. Ensure the existing save handler includes the new Shopify fields when calling updateSiteSettings mutation. Show success/error toasts on save completion

requirements.summary: Create Shopify Products Import UI within Admin page to fetch and import products from Shopify Storefront API
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Shopify Import' tab or panel appears in the Admin page when shopifyEnabled is true
When Shopify credentials are missing or integration is disabled, a prompt to configure settings is shown instead
Products are fetched from the Shopify Storefront GraphQL API using the stored domain and access token
Each product card shows title, thumbnail image, and price
Clicking 'Import' on a product card calls addProduct on the backend with the product data and shows a success or error toast
A loading state is shown while products are being fetched from Shopify

file_operations.description: Create utility module for interacting with Shopify Storefront GraphQL API. Export fetchShopifyProducts function that accepts storeDomain and storefrontAccessToken, constructs the Storefront API endpoint URL, and sends a GraphQL query to fetch products with fields: id, title, description, priceRange, images, productType. Return typed array of Shopify product objects. Include error handling for network failures and invalid credentials

Create panel component that: (1) accepts siteSettings as prop and checks if shopifyEnabled is true and credentials are configured; (2) if not configured, displays a message prompting admin to configure Shopify settings with a link to the Site Settings tab; (3) if configured, fetches products from Shopify using the fetchShopifyProducts utility and displays them in a responsive grid; (4) for each product, renders a card with thumbnail image, title, formatted price, and an 'Import' button; (5) on Import click, calls the importShopifyProduct backend method (note: verify backend method signature matches Product fields) with mapped Shopify data and shows success/error toast; (6) shows loading skeleton while fetching products from Shopify; (7) handles empty state when no products are returned

Add useImportShopifyProduct mutation hook that calls the backend importShopifyProduct method. Accept parameters: title, category, description, price, imageUrl, stock, featured. Invalidate the products query cache on success to refresh the product list

Add a new 'Shopify Import' tab to the Tabs component navigation alongside 'Products' and 'Site Settings'. In the corresponding TabsContent, render the ShopifyImportPanel component passing the current siteSettings from the useSiteSettings query hook. Ensure the tab is always visible in the UI (users will see a configuration prompt if Shopify is not enabled)
